title: Cloud Run Revisions, Validation, Traffic, Rollback, and Recovery · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Deployment Iam Separation
summary: A pipeline deploys latest, routes all traffic before readiness and smoke checks, changes IAM with application release, cannot identify the prior digest, and calls redeployment a rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci deployment iam separation increment in progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.
Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Deployment Iam Separation · Theory Ci Deployment Iam Separation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each. A defensible solution applies Ci Deployment Iam Separation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-2
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-2
    artifact: cloud-run-delivery.yaml
    decision: worked cd cloud run progressive delivery 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      deploy-cloudrun-action: 3
      service: status-api
      region: us-central1
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      revision-suffix: run8842
      traffic: none-at-deploy
      smoke: authenticated-changed-case
      canary-percent: 5
      observe: latency-errors-user-success
      abort: explicit-threshold
      rollback: prior-revision-traffic
      iam: separately-owned
    verify: inspect revision image identity readiness authenticated smoke traffic totals observation window abort decision rollback and IAM audit
    failure: Reject latest tags, deploy acceptance as readiness, instant full traffic, representative-canary assumptions, rebuild rollback, or deployment-owned public IAM.
    repair: Deploy verified digest without traffic, smoke exact revision, canary with signals, abort predictably, move traffic back, and separate IAM.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The deployment workflow should automatically make a service public for convenience. This fails the Ci Deployment Iam Separation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.
